frameworks.name: generative sequence sampling
frameworks.one_line: Internally generated state sequences support exploration, consolidation, planning

frameworks.name: entorhinal-hippocampal circuit model
frameworks.one_line: MEC dorsoventral grid input modulates hippocampal reactivation regimes

core_question: How can the entorhinal-hippocampal system flexibly adapt internally generated sequences to support different cognitive functions?
key_assumptions: Different cognitive functions require qualitatively distinct sampling dynamics
Grid population input scales systematically along MEC dorsoventral axis
Hippocampal reactivations reflect generative sampling processes
manipulations: Modulation of grid input scale along MEC dorsoventral axis
key_findings: Modulating MEC grid input interpolates between distinct hippocampal reactivation regimes
Model reproduces generative cycling, diffusive reactivations, and jumping trajectory events
interpretations: Dorsoventral grid modulation provides a single mechanism flexibly tuning sequence statistics
Apparently unrelated reactivation phenomena reflect one common generative process
